titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Meller collection of John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale papers
Meller, (James) collection of John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Meg Rinn

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 15, 2025 at 18:00

did.unittitle: James Meller collection of John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale papers

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

did.abstract: The collection comprises correspondence with John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale, copies of articles written by the couple, conference reports, and related reprints of articles that reflect James Meller's interest in futurism and professional relationship with John McHale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises James Meller's letters from John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale, copies of articles written by McHale and Cordell McHale, conference reports, and related reprints of articles that reflect James Meller's interest in futurism and professional relationship with John McHale. The bulk of the collection focuses on articles and conference reports.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Gift of the Richard Saltoun Gallery

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged into the series: Series I. Articles and reports; Series II. Correspondence; Series III. Printed material.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Meller collection of John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale papers. Yale Center for British Art, Archives.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Archives Department.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Meller was an associate of Buckminster Fuller and interested in futurism. He edited The Buckminster Fuller Reader , published in 1970.

Magda Cordell McHale (1921 June 24 - 2008 February 21) was a Hungarian born artist and professor. As a founding member of the Independent Group, she explored the place of popular culture in art, laying the foundation for Pop Art in Britain. Her work was often expressionist in manner, focused on women's bodies.

She moved with her second husband, John McHale, to the United States in 1962. The two wrote extensively about futurism, including the role of technology, commuication, women, and ecology, eventually founding the Center for Integrative Studies. The Center continued after John's death in 1978, establishing a presence at the State University of New York Buffalo, where Magda would become a part of the faculty until her retirement in 1999.

John McHale (1922 August 19 - 1978 November 2) was a Scottish artist, as well as future studies researcher, art theorist, and sociologist. He was among the founding members of the Independent Group, a group of artists who interrogated the place of popular culture in art, paving the way for Pop Art in Britian. His preferred artistic medium was collage.

McHale moved with his second wife, Magda Cordell McHale, to the United States in 1962. The move was prompted by the opportunity to work with Buckminster Fuller on issues related to the environment. He completed a PhD at the State University of New York Binghamton, where he founded the first version of the Center for Integrative Studies with Magda. He published extensively about futrism related topics such as the environment, technology, women, and communication, frequently in collaboration with Magda. He died in 1978.

did.unittitle: Series I. Articles and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains articles and conference reports gathered by James Meller written either by John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale or reflecting their active involvement. It also contains two pieces of correspondence that were originally interfiled with the other material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is in original order, which is roughly in reverse chronological order. It is listed in fully reversed chronological order.

did.unittitle: Magda Cordell McHale letter to James Meller
"In Memoriam: John McHale." Royal Institute of British Architects Journal
Ray Fletcher letter to James Meller
Leonard, John. "Private Lives." The New York Times (New York, NY)
Educational futures: individual, societal, global perspectives
McHale, John. "Forecasting and futures research." Publication unknown.
McHale, John. "Beyond the Exponentials." Publication unknown.
McHale, John and Magda Cordell McHale. "Basic Human Needs and Sustainable Growth." The Futurist
McHale, John. "Systems Simulation - Man Plus.' Behavioral Science vol. 20 no. 1
Subject - possible volspkr John McHale
McHale, John, Magda Cordell McHale. "The timetable project, stage 1: an assessment of projected relationships between populations and resources" Ekistics , vol. 37 no. 221
Working draft copy, The timetable project[…]
Typological survey of Futures Research by John McHale
McHale, John. "World energy resources in the future." Futures vol. 1 no. 1
McHale, John. "Global Ecology: toward the planetary society." The American Behavioral Scientist
Minutes of the Advisory Council meeting for the Joint Conference of the American Geographical Society and the World Academy of Art and Science, American Division
Summary minutes of general discussion advisory council (first day)
Interdisciplinary coordination working group report
McHale, John. "A Global View." Social Policies in the Seventies: nine divergent views edited by Robert Theobald, Doubleday and Company
McHale, John. "Toward the Future." The Teilhard Review vol. 3 no. 1
McHale, John. "2000+." Ekistics , vol. 24 no. 142
"World Resources inventory uses, 'total systems approach'" [extract], The Futurist
McHale, John. "On the ecology of political violence: 'the long hot summer' as hypothesis." American Behavioral Scientist Vol. XI no. 6
McHale, John. "The future of "the future" will be what we make of it." The Futurist vol. 1 no. 3
Conference on the Planetary Society
World literacy re: world problems
McHale, John. "World University - a global state of mind." Matrix '67
Report of the developments since the conference of overseas sponsors held in London in November 1965
McHale, John. "Education for real." Publication unknown
McHale, John. "Sharing Not Enough….world literacy on global problems.", Society
Levels of Technology [extract] from unidentified publication
Educational futures, Houston Style. Observations on the first confernece of the World Future Society's education section
McHale, John and Magda Cordell McHale. "Management: the larger perspective." Challenge to Leadership: managing in a changing world. The Free Press, New York. Collier-Macmillan publishers
McHale, John. "This Runaway World - can man gain control? Man Plus." The P. Teilhard de Chardin Association of Great Britain and Ireland
Series II. Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The correspondence series is primarily focused on communication between James Meller and John McHale, with some letters signed by both John and Magda and addressed to both James and his wife Gillian. Originally, John and James were in contact due to a mutual interest in the World Design Science Decade and future studies in general, as well their individual working relationships with Buckminster Fuller. John and James exchange book recommendations, articles, and other resources. There is also the occasional request from John to help the Center for Integrative Studies make a purchase. The correspondence tapers off around 1971, with references to phone calls suggesting that the primary form of communication changed.

did.unittitle: Envelopes and reports
Correspondence
Correspondence wrapper
Series III. Printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The printed material series contains publications by John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale along with works of others relevant to Meller's interest futurism.

did.unittitle: The Triple Revolution (reprint)
Science, Technology, and Change by John McHale
The Changing Pattern of Future Research in the USA by John McHale
Human Requirements, Supply Levels, and Outerbounds: a framework for thinking about the planetary bargin by John McHale and Magda Cordell McHale
The Planetary Bargin: proposals for a new inernational economic order to meet human needs, report of an international workshop convened in Aspen Colorado
Women in World Terms: facts and trends
International conference on the future of the past: change and cultural transformation
New Perspectives on Changing Relationships: Canda, Mexico, United States summary report
International conference on the future of the past: historical identity? And permanence and change
